title: Fix array instead of one elem network http api
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->
Closes #9690
Fixes  #9690

curl -v --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/networks/podman/json
**Describe the results you received:**
the array of one of network report objects.
**Describe the results you expected:**
One report object, since input guarantees there will only ever be one output.

This PR fixes the above issue.

comments.body: If there are some tests needed for this one, please suggest to me of what type.
Is this a potential API breaking change? @jwhonce @vrothberg @baude

> @jmguzik test/apiv2/35-networks.at will need to be adjusted as well

@jwhonce Added a test + aligned bindings and other files.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jmguzik*, *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
rdoproject job is failing but I think this one is ready (not related fail)
Did @mheon's API breakage question get answered?  At least a one or two-line description of the change should be provided...

@TomSweeneyRedHat from my perspective it is fixing API as described in issue #9690.
The current behavior is different than described here: https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodInspectNetwork

Quotes from issue:
> curl -v --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/networks/podman/json
> **Describe the results you received:**
> array of one of network report objects.
> **Describe the results you expected:**
> One report object, since input guarantees there will only ever be one output.

@jmguzik I concur.  However, when you're creating a PR, please also include at least a sentence or two saying what it's fixing.  Just links to an issue isn't enough IMHO, I don't want to have to hop around on links to find what the PR is trying to cure.  My $.02

> @jmguzik I concur. However, when you're creating a PR, please also include at least a sentence or two saying what it's fixing. Just links to an issue isn't enough IMHO, I don't want to have to hop around on links to find what the PR is trying to cure. My $.02

Noted and edited. In every open source project rules are different. I will try to remember about yours.
